email_1.name: Problem (Day 0)
email_1.body_template:
Hi [NAME],

Most [TITLE]s at [COMPANY] I talk to are dealing with [PROBLEM].

When that happens, [AGITATION].

[SOLUTION].

Worth a 15-min call this week?

[SENDER_NAME]
email_1.notes: Keep under 80 words. No pitch deck. One specific problem.

email_2.name: Social Proof (Day 3-4)
email_2.body_template:
Hi [NAME],

A [TITLE] at a similar company was dealing with [PROBLEM]. They [SOLUTION] and [RESULT].

Thought it might be relevant. Open to a quick chat?

[SENDER_NAME]
email_2.notes: Reference a real or plausible case study. One result metric if possible.

email_3.name: Value / Insight (Day 7-8)
email_3.body_template:
Hi [NAME],

[INSIGHT relevant to their role or industry — 1-2 sentences, genuinely useful].

Mentioned it because it's directly relevant to what you're working on at [COMPANY].

Happy to share how others are responding to this — just reply here.

[SENDER_NAME]
email_3.notes: No explicit ask. Pure value. This email re-opens the conversation.

email_4.name: Breakup (Day 11-12)
email_4.body_template:
Hi [NAME],

Haven't heard back — totally understand if now isn't the right time.

Should I close your file, or would a quick 15-min call still make sense?

Either way, no hard feelings.

[SENDER_NAME]
email_4.notes: Short, human, low-pressure. Creates urgency without being aggressive.
